privacyNotice.title: Privacy notice
privacyNotice.metaDescription: México Medieval privacy notice: use of contact data for the newsletter and site communications.
privacyNotice.p1: México Medieval, the website of Hervin Fernández Aceves, may collect contact data only when a person subscribes to the newsletter or communicates through the channels indicated on this site.
privacyNotice.p2: The data that may be collected are name and email address. In some cases, if the person chooses to provide them, institution, country, or contact message may also be collected.
privacyNotice.p3: Personal data will be used exclusively to send the México Medieval newsletter and, where applicable, to share information about courses, fairs, festivals, lectures, publications, and activities linked to the site.
privacyNotice.p4: The data provided will not be sold, rented, or used for purposes unrelated to the requested communication.
privacyNotice.p5: The newsletter may be sent through an external email management platform. In that case, the data will be processed only to manage the subscription and send the requested communications.
privacyNotice.p6: Subscribers may unsubscribe from the newsletter at any time through the link included in each email or by writing through the contact channels indicated on this site.
privacyNotice.p7Before: Interested persons may request access, rectification, cancellation, or opposition regarding their personal data through the contact channels indicated on the
privacyNotice.p8: This privacy notice may be updated to reflect changes to the site, the newsletter, or México Medieval activities. Any changes will be published on this page.

home.heroImgAlt: Illuminated manuscript with gold leaf and gothic calligraphy on aged parchment
home.heroKicker: History, culture, and medieval worlds for everyone
home.heroSub: The Middle Ages from Mexico: nobles and peasants, wars and books, saints and monsters, legends, power, and everyday life.
home.heroSubLineTwo: Courses, lectures, podcasts, and gatherings for discovering and enjoying the Middle Ages inside and outside the classroom.
home.booksDesc: Annotated primary texts and rare first editions from the New Spain press.
home.articlesDesc: Scholarly critiques on ecclesiastical architecture and colonial governance.
home.podcastDesc: Discussions with historians specializing in the syncretism of Medieval México.
home.eventsDesc: Lectures, appearances, and curated moving-image resources on medieval México and related scholarship.
home.papersDesc: Conference presentations and papers in scholarly repositories.
home.spotlightPodcastBody: Listen on Apple Podcasts or your favourite podcast app.

libros.heroAlt: Ancient parchment manuscript with illuminated initials
libros.subtitle: Books written and co-edited by Hervin Fernández Aceves on medieval history, Mediterranean worlds, power, society, and written culture.
libros.heroSub: Journey through the illuminated annals of México. From sacred liturgies to the maps that defined New Spain, explore the ink and spirit of five centuries.

podcastPage.subtitle: Original episodes and collaborations for approaching history, the humanities, and the Middle Ages.
podcastPage.cardAlt1: Medieval knight armor and sword on velvet
podcastPage.cardAlt2: Parchment scroll with wax seal on wooden table
podcastPage.heroSub: Our podcast series explores the profound connections between the medieval Mediterranean and the early modern Americas. We translate scholarly rigor into narrative journeys through time.
podcastPage.collaborationsBody: Appearances on other shows and podcasts about medieval history, public history, historical culture, and public conversation.
podcastPage.sectionDiasGarraTitle: Días con La Garra y Ale Garibay
podcastPage.appleSubtitle: A project of conversation and public history developed with Lancaster University and ARDEMAC.
podcastPage.sectionMexicoMedievalBody: The original season of México Medieval brings together episodes on historical reenactment, siege weapons, video games, medieval festivals, food, powerful women, the right of conquest, transitions of power, and medieval legends, designed to rediscover the Middle Ages from Mexico.
podcastPage.listLoadError: Could not load episodes. Please try again later.
podcastPage.sectionDiasGarraBody: Conversations with Dr Jabón / Hervin Fernández on Días con La Garra y Ale Garibay about history, culture, curiosities from Mexico and, since we are already here, the rest of humanity.

about.title: About us
about.subtitle: A brief page about the historian behind the project and the idea behind México Medieval.
about.projectLede: ‘México Medieval’ is a platform for thinking about, teaching, and sharing the Middle Ages from Mexico. It brings together academic projects, publications, courses, seminars, podcasts, fairs, festivals, and activities for both university audiences and people interested in history beyond the classroom.
about.projectLede2: The project seeks to bring medieval history closer without reducing it to castles, crusades, and the names of kings. Here, the Middle Ages appear as a broad field of questions about culture, power, society, religion, writing, historical imagination, and contemporary uses of the medieval past.
about.projectKicker: What does México Medieval bring together?
about.pillar1Body: Books, articles, chapters, and projects on medieval history, the Mediterranean, sources, power, and society.
about.pillar2Body: Academic and educational activities for studying the Middle Ages inside and outside university settings.
about.pillar3Body: Podcasts, fairs, festivals, lectures, and experiences for bringing medieval history to wider audiences.
about.authorTagline: Historian, medievalist, and researcher in the humanities and social sciences.
about.authorBio1: Hervin Fernández Aceves is a historian, medievalist, and researcher. He studied Political Science and Public Administration at UNAM, completed MAs in Comparative History and Interdisciplinary Medieval Studies at Central European University, and earned his PhD in History from the University of Leeds. He was awarded CONACYT fellowships abroad and held postdoctoral positions at the British School at Rome and Lancaster University.
about.authorBio2: His work sits at the intersection of social and political history, pre-modern studies, and digital humanities. He has published with Bloomsbury and Brill, as well as in academic journals in the United Kingdom, the United States, and Mexico. He is a Fellow of the Royal Historical Society and a National Researcher in Mexico’s SNII.
about.authorBio3: He is currently a part-time research professor at El Colegio de Jalisco and an adjunct lecturer at Universidad Panamericana. He is also the editor-in-chief of Esfera Pública and Letras Históricas, and contributes to public history projects, radio, and podcasts to bring medieval history to wider audiences.
about.theme1: Kingdoms and nobility in medieval Italy
